steps.title: Use the given arc
steps.content:
The arc with central angle $100^\circ$ has length $5\pi$. Use the arc length formula to connect that arc to the full circumference:
$$5\pi=\frac{100}{360}(2\pi r)$$

steps.title: Find the circumference
steps.content:
Solve for the full circumference $2\pi r$:
$$5\pi=\frac{5}{18}(2\pi r)$$
$$2\pi r=18\pi$$

steps.title: Subtract the other arc
steps.content:
The two arcs from $A$ to $C$ make the full circle. Arc $ADC$ is $5\pi$, so arc $ABC$ is the remaining part:
$$18\pi-5\pi=13\pi$$
The answer is B.
